---
title: "Research"
pagetitle: "Research"
page-layout: article
comments: false
sidebar: false
---

My research lies at the intersection of formal semantics and syntax. I am particularly interested in how modal meaning relates to the syntactic properties of complements.

## Modal ellipsis and anaphora

My current work examines modal ellipsis and anaphora in French. I use these phenomena to investigate the relationship between modal flavor, especially epistemic and non-epistemic interpretations, and complement structure.

My approach is primarily theoretical, complemented by experimental work. More broadly, I am interested in how experimental evidence can inform formal analyses of meaning and grammatical structure.

## Selected work

### French Modal Ellipsis and Anaphora: Testing the Epistemic Restriction

This project tests the claim that French modal ellipsis and anaphora are unavailable with epistemic interpretations of *devoir*. It presents evidence from an acceptability-judgment experiment with 213 participants.

Presented at **Experiments in Linguistic Meaning 4 (ELM 4)**.

[Read the abstract](https://elm-conference.net/wp-content/uploads/ELM4/Abstracts/paper144.pdf)

## Broader interests

I also have strong interests in logic, philosophy, psycholinguistics, and neurolinguistics. I am pursuing the NACS certificate alongside my PhD.
